title: Ballard Power Systems Q1 2026 Results
text: Ballard Power Systems reported Q1 2026 revenue of $19.4 million, representing 26% year-over-year growth. Revenue diversification showed Bus at $6.8 million (down 46% YoY), Rail at $5.1 million (up 4,472% YoY), Stationary at $5.2 million (up 775% YoY), and Other Markets at $2.4 million (up 6% YoY). Gross margin improved significantly to 14%, a 37-point improvement from Q1 2025. Operating expenses declined 36% to $16.4 million. Adjusted EBITDA improved to negative $11.4 million from negative $27.5 million in the prior year. Cash position ended at $516.8 million, down from $576.7 million year-over-year. CEO Marty Neese stated: We ended Q1 with $516.8 million in cash and no near- or mid-term financing requirements. Operating cash burn improved 68% to $7.8 million. Order backlog was $112.9 million, down 5% from Q4 2025, while the 12-month orderbook was $52.8 million. The company noted positive cash flow delivery in Q1 reflecting the early impact of ongoing transformation initiatives. Revenue in 2026 is expected to be back-half weighted with no specific guidance provided.
